1.title: Who is like the Lord?
1.text: The name Micah is short for a Hebrew question, 'Who is like the Lord?' — a theme the prophet returns to in his very last chapter (7:18).

1.question: According to Micah 1:5, what is the transgression of Jacob identified with?
In Micah 1:16, the people are told to make themselves bald because their children have gone into what?

2.question: In Micah 2:2, what do the wicked covet and take by violence?

2.template: The women of my people have ye cast out from their pleasant {0}

2.title: A prophet of wine
2.text: Micah 2:11 mocks the people for preferring a false prophet who would 'prophesy of wine and of strong drink' — a preacher of comfort, not of repentance.

2.question: How does Micah 2:12 say the Lord will gather the remnant of Israel?
options: As sheep in the midst of their fold

3.title: Full of power by the Spirit
3.text: Against the corrupt prophets, Micah declares in 3:8 that he is 'full of power by the spirit of the Lord' — the true prophet's authority comes from God, not from pay.

3.question: In Micah 3:5, what do the false prophets cry when they are fed, but declare against those who feed them not?

3.template: shall {0} for your sake be plowed as a field

4.title: Swords into plowshares
4.text: Micah 4:3 — 'they shall beat their swords into plowshares' — is inscribed on a wall facing the United Nations in New York, and appears almost word for word in Isaiah 2.

4.question: In Micah 4:3, into what will the nations beat their swords?

4.template: we will walk in the name of the {0} our God for ever and ever

4.question: In Micah 4:9, the daughter of Zion is seized with pangs like whom?
In Micah 4:13, the Lord tells the daughter of Zion to arise and do what?

5.template: But thou, {0} Ephratah, though thou be little among the thousands of Judah

5.title: The birthplace of the Messiah
5.text: When the wise men asked where the Christ should be born, the chief priests answered from Micah 5:2 — Bethlehem — even though the town was 'little among the thousands of Judah.'

5.question: In Micah 5:5, who is said to become 'the peace' when the Assyrian comes into the land?
In Micah 5:9, what does the Lord promise concerning the enemies of his people?
In Micah 5:15, upon whom will the Lord execute vengeance in anger and fury?
options: The nations that did not obey

6.question: In Micah 6:4, whom did the Lord send before Israel when he brought them out of Egypt?
In Micah 6:5, the people are told to remember what Balak king of Moab consulted, and the answer of whom?

6.template: what doth the Lord require of thee, but to do justly, and to love {0}

6.title: Justice, mercy, and humility
6.text: Micah 6:8 boils the whole law down to three things — to do justly, love mercy, and walk humbly with God — one of the most quoted verses in the Old Testament.

6.question: In Micah 6:10, what does the Lord condemn in the house of the wicked?
options: Treasures of wickedness and a scant measure

7.title: Enemies within the household
7.text: Micah 7:6 — 'a man's enemies are the men of his own house' — was quoted by Jesus in Matthew 10:36 to describe how the gospel can divide even families.

7.question: In Micah 7:2, what does the prophet lament has perished out of the earth?
In Micah 7:8, what does the prophet declare even though he has fallen?
In Micah 7:19, what does the Lord do with the sins of his people?
options: Casts them into the depths of the sea
